howItWorks.intro: This music streaming revenue calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Gross Revenue = Monthly Streams x Per-Stream Rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does Spotify pay per stream?
faq.answer: Spotify pays approximately $0.003-0.005 per stream, averaging about $0.004. The exact rate varies by country, listener subscription type (free vs premium), and total platform streams that month.

faq.question: Which streaming platform pays the most?
faq.answer: Tidal pays the highest per-stream rate ($0.008-0.013), followed by Apple Music ($0.007-0.01). However, Spotify has by far the most users, so total earnings often favor Spotify despite lower per-stream rates.

faq.question: Is the Music Streaming Revenue Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard music and audio formulas trusted by musicians and audio engineers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What monthly streams should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate monthly streams value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Music Streaming Revenue Calculator?
sections.body: The Music Streaming Revenue Calculator is a free online tool designed for musicians and audio engineers who need quick, accurate calculations in the music and audio space. By entering your monthly streams, platform, distributor cut, you get instant results including gross monthly revenue, net monthly, estimated annual revenue. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting gross monthly revenue right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In music and audio, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by musicians and audio engineers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to fine-tune your sound with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your monthly streams and need to find the right gross monthly revenue. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Monthly Streams
howToSteps.description: Type or select your monthly streams in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Platform
howToSteps.description: Type or select your platform in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Distributor Cut (%)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your distributor cut in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your gross monthly revenue, net monthly, estimated annual revenue. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your monthly streams before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both gross monthly revenue and net monthly — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to fine-tune your sound.
If you're unsure about your distributor cut, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
